sourceMovie.title: Midsommar
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Daylight horror in Swedish pastoral drag—grief, bad relationships, and rituals that start weird and end unforgettable. Aster’s folk nightmare is as funny as it is upsetting.

recommendations.title: The Witch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Historical dread, isolation, and a family unraveling in nature’s indifference. If Midsommar’s folk texture hooked you, Eggers’ Puritan nightmare is the obvious next ritual.

recommendations.title: The Wicker Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The blueprint for ‘nice villagers, terrible agenda’ horror—quirky, creepy, and ending on a note you won’t shake.

recommendations.title: Joker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Not horror, but shared DNA in mental fracture, social pressure, and discomfort-as-vibe. If you liked Midsommar’s relationship rot, Phoenix’s spiral rhymes.

recommendations.title: The Lighthouse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Two men, a rock, madness in monochrome. Eggers again—claustrophobic, mythic, and darkly funny in the same way Midsommar winks before it hurts you.

recommendations.title: Hereditary
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Aster’s first family grief grenade—more domestic, equally traumatic. If Midsommar’s relationship dynamics felt too real, you’ve been warned.

recommendations.title: Suspiria
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dance academy cults, grotesque set pieces, and a hypnotic score. Arty, loud, and committed—good if you liked Midsommar’s communal dread.

recommendations.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Clinical dialogue and impossible choices—Lanthimos dread with a mythic title. Unsettling in a flat-affect way that pairs with Aster’s controlled tone.

recommendations.title: The Black Phone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: More conventional horror thrills, but strong craft and kid-in-peril tension. A palate cleanser if you still want dread without folk pageantry.

recommendations.title: Titane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Body horror that refuses categorization—provocative, tender, and wild. For viewers who want Midsommar-level ‘I can’t believe I’m watching this’ energy.

recommendations.title: Barbarian
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A rental-house thriller that keeps changing genres on you—funny, nasty, and designed for audiences who yell at the screen.

faq.question: Is Midsommar scarier than Hereditary?
faq.answer: Taste varies: Hereditary is often felt as more relentlessly grim at home; Midsommar’s daylight and communal horror can feel eerier in a different register.

faq.question: What movies are like Midsommar but less disturbing?
faq.answer: The Witch is still intense but slower; if you want lighter folk flavor without trauma-core, consider something like The Village (knowing its twist debate).

faq.question: Why is Midsommar called a breakup movie?
faq.answer: Aster frames relationship dysfunction as loudly as the cult plot—many viewers read the final act as a grotesque metaphor for emotional release and agency.
